title: parallax cross-model review ledger (per-slice)
description: Per-SLICE memory of the cross-model verifier's findings across review rounds. Lives at .parallax/<slug>/reviews/<slice_id>.json (one file per slice, so a slice cannot spend another's review budget). It is written ONLY by scripts/merge-ledger.py from the verifier's review-round output — never hand-edited by the orchestrating Claude (the producer must not author its own certification). It carries NO trusted policy: scripts/triage.py reads the [review] policy from .parallax/codex.toml, never from here. See commands/run.md -> 'Review memory, rounds & disposition'.

slice_id.description: The slice this ledger belongs to (e.g. S1). One ledger file per slice.

round_receipts.description: v0.38 5.3 (gate A4) — one receipt per verifier round, appended by merge-ledger.py AFTER it persisted the verbatim provider response as <slice_id>.round<N>.raw.json next to this ledger (symmetric with pre-freeze). A post-green verdict with no re-readable raw receipt does not exist: triage.py refuses a ledger whose receipts do not cover rounds_used, and epic-gate.py re-reads each committed raw file, sha256-matches it against the receipt, and schema-validates it as a review round — so a hand-authored 'pass' extracted from a malformed envelope (the RUN1 S2 live failure) can no longer reach a gate.

rounds_used.description: Verifier review rounds consumed for THIS slice (one per merge-ledger.py call; the initial post-green review is round 1). [review].max_rounds caps it; at the cap, live blockers PARK. This is the single source of truth for the round budget — run-state only points at this file, it does not keep a second counter.

policy_hash.description: OPTIONAL audit-only sha of the [review] table this ledger was triaged under. Advisory; triage NEVER reads policy from the ledger. (There is deliberately no `policy` object here — a policy-bearing ledger is rejected, fail-closed.) Recorded by merge-ledger --policy, FROZEN per run, checked by epic-gate.py against the committed policy.

contract_hash.description: OPTIONAL frozen hash of the normative spec contract (.parallax/<slug>/{spec.md,slices.md,validation.md,slices.lock}) this slice was verified AGAINST (scripts/contract-hash.sh). Recorded by merge-ledger --contract-hash, FROZEN per run, and recomputed+checked by epic-gate.py from the pinned feature commit — so the spec/validation can't be rewritten after review (v0.26 P0).

findings.description: Every finding ever raised for this slice, with its lifecycle. Never deleted — settled (status=fixed, codex-verified) or contested (claude_rebuttal). That history is the regression memory.

id.description: Stable id, e.g. S2-N3 (slice + finding number), assigned by merge-ledger.py.

fingerprint.description: sha256(kind | spec_ref | file(where))[:16] — keys the SAME defect to the SAME id across rounds (stable across rephrasing and line shifts).

spec_ref.description: REQUIRED — the spec section/anchor the finding is grounded in. A finding with no spec reference is inadmissible.

where.description: Location (file[:line]); the file part feeds the fingerprint.

claim.description: What is wrong, in one concrete sentence.

evidence.description: Concrete, independently-checkable evidence (file:line, input, observed vs spec'd).

status.description: open = live; fixed = the INDEPENDENT model verified the fix against last_verified_diff; regressed = a previously-fixed finding the verifier reproduced again (live).

functional_repro.description: True if a reproducible functional error — always blocks regardless of severity.

verified_by.description: Who verified a `fixed`. MUST be "codex" for a fix to count as settled — Claude cannot self-assert a fix (triage treats a non-codex `fixed` as still live).

resolution.description: How it was resolved (the verifier's note), or why contested.

claude_rebuttal.description: Set ONLY when Claude contests. A contested BLOCKING finding ESCALATES (human decides); it is never silently dropped or auto-greened. Ignored for always-block kinds and reproducible functional errors.

round.description: Round in which this finding was first raised.

last_verified_diff.description: Assembled-diff SHA the status was last checked against. For a `fixed` finding triage requires this to equal the CURRENT diff, else the fix is stale and the finding is live again.
